vocab.meaning: làm cho túi xách bừa bộn/chật chội
vocab.example: I try to keep my essentials to a minimum, so I'd rather not clutter up my bag with a physical mirror.

vocab.meaning: kiểm tra hình ảnh phản chiếu (soi gương)
vocab.example: I don't carry a mirror because there are plenty of glass windows downtown to quickly check my reflection.

vocab.meaning: bận tâm quá mức/ám ảnh về cái gì
vocab.example: I'm not someone who is constantly preoccupied with my physical appearance while out and about.

vocab.meaning: một sự cần thiết thực tế
vocab.example: For professional makeup artists, carrying a portable mirror is more of a practical necessity than a choice.

vocab.meaning: duy trì vẻ ngoài chỉn chu/hoàn hảo
vocab.example: I only bring a small mirror along when I need to maintain a polished look for a wedding or a gala.
I find it easy to use any reflective surface, like a blank computer screen, if I need to adjust my hair.
I usually opt for convenience and just use my phone's camera instead of carrying an actual mirror.

vocab.meaning: thói quen chăm sóc diện mạo cá nhân
vocab.example: My personal grooming habits are quite low-maintenance, so a mirror isn't a permanent fixture in my purse.

vocab.meaning: cồng kềnh/vướng víu khi mang theo
vocab.example: Even the smallest glass mirrors can be a bit cumbersome to carry in a slim clutch bag.

vocab.meaning: trong tầm tay/gần sát bên mình
vocab.example: I rarely have a mirror within arm's reach since I prefer to focus on my surroundings rather than my face.
